findings.purpose: Provides semantic similarity search over Korean skincare ingredient names inside the backend-fastapi-copy FastAPI service. The subsystem embeds all known ingredients from a PostgreSQL product_analysis table using a local KLUE-BERT-base model, stores the resulting vectors as a JSON file (locally and on AWS S3), and answers ingredient-filter queries by cosine similarity at runtime.
findings.architecture: The embedding subsystem is a single class, IngredientMatcher (app/services/filtering.py), exposed to the wider app through a global singleton factory (get_ingredient_matcher). On first instantiation it: (1) downloads the KLUE-BERT-base model from S3 or loads it from app/data/models/klue-bert-base/ if already cached; (2) loads the pre-computed embedding dictionary from app/data/embeddings/ingredient_embeddings.json or downloads it from S3 path models/data/ingredient-search/ingredient_embeddings.json. The in-memory embedding store is a plain Python dict mapping ingredient name string -> torch.Tensor of shape [768]. At query time, the query text is tokenized (max_length=64), passed through the model, mean-pooled, L2-normalized, and cosine-compared against every entry in the dict; results above a 0.9 threshold are returned sorted descending. An exact-substring-in-ingredient match applies a +0.2 boost capped at 1.0. The ingestion/refresh path (compute_and_save_embeddings) fetches DISTINCT matched_answer values from the product_analysis table where question_id='CM00aa7ace476c60', processes them in batches of 32, writes two local JSON files (timestamped archive + canonical ingredient_embeddings.json), updates the singleton's in-memory dict, and uploads both to S3. There is no pgvector or ANN index — similarity search is a full linear scan over the dict. The subsystem does NOT use a postgres vector column; embeddings live in flat JSON files.

keyCommands.purpose: Triggers compute_and_save_embeddings: re-queries product_analysis, re-embeds all ingredients in batches of 32, writes JSON locally and uploads to S3, updates in-memory singleton.
Returns fast prefix/substring SQL matches from product_analysis. Does NOT use the vector embeddings — returns up to 10 results with hardcoded similarity scores 1.000 (prefix) or 0.900 (substring).

notableDecisions.decision: Embeddings stored as flat JSON (dict of name -> float[]), not in a vector database or pgvector column
notableDecisions.rationale: Simplifies deployment and avoids a dedicated vector store dependency. Enables S3-based versioning and timestamped archives of past embedding runs. The tradeoff is O(n) linear scan on every query; acceptable at the current corpus size (~6,256 ingredients).

notableDecisions.decision: Global singleton IngredientMatcher loaded once at first request, not at startup
notableDecisions.rationale: Model loading is expensive (~seconds). The singleton pattern avoids per-request overhead while lazy initialization avoids blocking FastAPI startup if S3 is unreachable.

notableDecisions.decision: KLUE-BERT-base (Korean BERT) as the embedding model instead of a multilingual or English model
notableDecisions.rationale: Ingredient names in this dataset are Korean strings; KLUE-BERT-base is specifically pre-trained on Korean text. The model is downloaded from S3 rather than HuggingFace Hub in production to avoid external network calls.

notableDecisions.decision: Exact-match substring boost (+0.2) on top of cosine similarity
notableDecisions.rationale: Pure cosine similarity can under-rank ingredients whose name literally contains the query string. The boost ensures prefix/exact matches surface first.

notableDecisions.decision: hybrid-search API endpoint uses pure SQL (LIKE), not the vector embeddings
notableDecisions.rationale: Prefix/substring SQL is faster for typeahead scenarios; the semantic fallback was apparently planned (comment says 'triggers background task for semantic search if needed') but not implemented.

findings.currentStatus: active — last commit touching subsystem files: 2026-02-23
findings.dataFlow: startup → IngredientMatcher.__init__() checks app/data/models/klue-bert-base/ → if missing, downloads model files from S3 (models/klue-bert-base/) → checks app/data/embeddings/ingredient_embeddings.json → if missing, downloads from S3 (models/data/ingredient-search/ingredient_embeddings.json) → loads dict into self._embeddings as {str: torch.Tensor[768]}. Query path: FilterProducts._process_ingredient_filter(ingredient) → IngredientMatcher.search_ingredients(query, threshold=0.9) → _get_embedding(query): tokenize+model+mean_pool+L2_norm → cosine_similarity against every entry in _embeddings + optional substring boost → list of {ingredient, similarity} above threshold → SQL lookup on product_analysis for matching group_ids. Refresh path: POST /ingredients/update-embeddings → compute_and_save_embeddings(session) → SELECT DISTINCT matched_answer FROM product_analysis WHERE question_id='CM00aa7ace476c60' → batch-embed (batch_size=32, max_length=64) → write timestamped JSON + ingredient_embeddings.json locally → upload both to S3 → update in-memory self._embeddings.

contentPointers.claim: kind: service — the subsystem is a runtime embedding service embedded in the FastAPI app, not a standalone CLI tool or pipeline script
displayName: Ingredient Embedding Service
tagline: Korean ingredient name embedding and similarity search backed by KLUE-BERT-base and AWS S3

sourcePath: app/services/filtering.py — the most representative single file; contains IngredientMatcher class (model loading, embedding generation, similarity search, refresh pipeline) and FilterProducts wrapper
tags: [embeddings, nlp, korean, bert, similarity-search, aws-s3, fastapi, skincare, ingredients]
techStack.primary: [Python, FastAPI]; infra: [AWS S3, PostgreSQL]; libs: [transformers==4.48.1, torch==2.5.1+cpu, boto3==1.35.66, huggingface-hub==0.28.0]

dependencies: IngredientMatcher depends on app/core/config.py (get_settings → AWS credentials, S3 client, S3 bucket name), app/core/table_resolver.py (TableResolver.product_analysis — dynamic versioned table name), postgres table product_analysis (column matched_answer, filter question_id='CM00aa7ace476c60'), AWS S3 bucket (model and embeddings storage), local filesystem paths app/data/models/klue-bert-base/ and app/data/embeddings/ingredient_embeddings.json
mechanism — embedding model: KLUE-BERT-base (klue/klue-bert-base), loaded via transformers AutoTokenizer + AutoModel; 768-dimensional output confirmed from embedding file inspection
contentPointers.evidence: filtering.py:38,88-89; embedding file count confirmed: 6256 ingredients, dim=768

contentPointers.claim: mechanism — mean pooling: token embeddings weighted by attention_mask, summed and divided by clamped mask sum, then L2-normalized with F.normalize(p=2, dim=1)
mechanism — similarity search: full linear scan with torch.cosine_similarity; default threshold=0.9; exact substring match adds +0.2 boost capped at 1.0; results sorted descending

mechanism — ingestion/refresh: fetches DISTINCT matched_answer from product_analysis WHERE question_id='CM00aa7ace476c60', processes in batch_size=32, tokenizes with max_length=64, writes timestamped JSON archive + canonical ingredient_embeddings.json locally, uploads both to S3
mechanism — model + embedding bootstrap: local cache checked first (model_dir/config.json, embedding_dir/ingredient_embeddings.json); S3 download is fallback only
mechanism — singleton: get_ingredient_matcher() returns a module-level _ingredient_matcher_instance; IngredientMatcher is instantiated once per process, not per request
API surface: POST /metrics/ingredients/update-embeddings triggers refresh pipeline; GET /metrics/ingredients/hybrid-search uses pure SQL (LIKE prefix/substring), NOT the vector embeddings
status.stage: active; status.lastCommitAt: 2026-02-23
known issue: hybrid-search endpoint docstring says it triggers semantic search 'if needed' but the semantic fallback is not implemented — the endpoint returns SQL substring results only
contentPointers.evidence: metrics.py:916-917 (comment), 940-951 (implementation: substring SQL, no embedding call)

contentPointers.claim: known issue: no scheduled or event-driven refresh — embeddings are refreshed only on explicit POST to /ingredients/update-embeddings; corpus drift between DB and embedding store is possible
contentPointers.evidence: filtering.py:595-597; scheduler.py has no reference to embed or ingredient

contentPointers.claim: no pgvector column or ANN index — embeddings are flat JSON, similarity is O(n) linear scan
contentPointers.evidence: filtering.py:324-327; alembic/versions/* contains no vector/embedding migration

contentPointers.claim: scope.inDoc: KLUE-BERT-base model loading and caching, embedding generation pipeline (compute_and_save_embeddings), in-memory embedding store, cosine similarity search with substring boost, S3 storage and retrieval, API endpoints for refresh and hybrid-search, FilterProducts integration

gapsFromBrief: Brief asked about 'caching or batching around the model API' — there is no external model API call at query time; the model runs locally (CPU). The only external call during refresh is S3 upload. No rate-limit handling exists because there is no API to rate-limit.

Brief asked about 'embedding dimension' — confirmed 768 by inspecting the embedding file directly, but the dimension is not hardcoded in filtering.py itself; it is implicitly set by the KLUE-BERT-base architecture.
The S3 bucket name is runtime-configured via env var S3_BUCKET_NAME — not visible in source. Could not confirm the exact bucket name without reading .env (skipped per constraint).
No explicit test coverage for the IngredientMatcher class was found (test.py exists at project root but was not read to stay within depth budget; the brief did not ask about tests).
recommendations: The hybrid-search endpoint has a misleading docstring ('triggers background task for semantic search if needed') but the semantic path is never called. The content-agent should flag this as a known issue with severity: medium.

The in-memory dict grows with corpus size; at 6,256 ingredients with 768-float vectors, each tensor is ~24KB, total ~150MB. This is within acceptable range now but worth noting if corpus grows significantly.
No scheduled refresh means embeddings can drift from the DB. A cron-triggered call to the update-embeddings endpoint (or a startup hook) would close this gap.
